title: Roasted Sweet Potatoes
subtitle: Crispy outside, soft inside, and easy to make plain or with a warm spice blend that keeps the natural sweetness front and center.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Choose one version per batch. Red Garnet or Jewel sweet potatoes roast especially well here.
items: Plain version: 1 lb sweet potatoes, about 3 large, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 tbsp melted coconut oil
3/8 tsp fine sea salt (use less if using table salt)
1/2 tbsp light brown sugar, firmly packed
Seasoned version: 1 lb sweet potatoes, about 3 large, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 tbsp olive oil
3/8 tsp fine sea salt (use less if using table salt)
1/8 tsp black pepper
1/4 tsp ground chili powder
1/4 tsp paprika
1/4 tsp ground cumin
1/4 tsp garlic powder
ingredients.callout: Best texture tip: Use your largest sheet pan and keep the potatoes in a single layer. Overlap = steaming instead of roasting.

body.html: Roasted sweet potato cubes with caramelized edges and creamy centers. You can keep them plain for meal prep or go seasoned with chili powder, paprika, cumin, and garlic.

items: Peel and cube the potatoes into even 1/2-inch pieces
Choose a flavor path: plain-sweet or warm-spiced
Spread out on a large sheet pan with no overlap
Flip every 10-15 minutes for even browning
Roast 27-35 minutes until crisp outside and tender inside

body.html: Key rule: Space matters. Crowded sweet potatoes steam and soften; well-spaced potatoes caramelize.

slides.title: Preheat + Prep
slides.checkboxLabel: Heat oven and cube potatoes evenly

items: Preheat oven to 425F.
Peel the sweet potatoes.
Cut into 1/2-inch pieces so everything roasts at the same pace.
Set out your largest sheet pan so the potatoes can roast in one layer.

body.html: Even cuts matter: Smaller pieces can burn before larger pieces finish, so aim for a consistent dice.

slides.title: Choose the Plain Version
slides.checkboxLabel: Toss with coconut oil, salt, and brown sugar

items: Add the sweet potatoes to the sheet pan.
Top with melted coconut oil, fine sea salt, and light brown sugar.
Toss until every piece is coated.
Spread into an even layer with no overlap.

body.html: Why this works: A touch of brown sugar helps the edges caramelize without overpowering the sweet potato flavor.

slides.title: Or Make the Seasoned Version
slides.checkboxLabel: Toss with oil and spice blend

items: Add the sweet potatoes to the sheet pan.
Top with olive oil, fine sea salt, black pepper, chili powder, paprika, cumin, and garlic powder.
Toss until the spice blend is evenly distributed.
Spread into an even layer with space between pieces.

body.html: Flavor option: Use chipotle chili powder and/or smoked paprika for a smokier roast. Add 1/4 to 1/2 tsp cayenne if you want real heat.

items: Roast for 27-35 minutes total.
Flip the potatoes every 10-15 minutes so they brown evenly and do not scorch on one side.
Aim for edges that are crispy and caramelized with centers that are soft and creamy.
A total bake time of about 30 minutes is common, but judge by color and tenderness.

body.html: Storage: Cool leftovers, then refrigerate in an airtight container up to 4 days. Reheat in the oven or air fryer to bring back the crisp edges.
